title: Relevance Judgments and Retrieval Metrics · Contract Workshop
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build retrieval evaluation notebook and judgment policy, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of retrieval evaluation notebook and judgment policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Define binary or graded relevance, assessor instructions, pooling, disagreement, unjudged handling, and query-specific evidence needs.
Compute precision, recall, F-score, success, and hit rate at an explicit cutoff and explain which failure each reveals.
Compute reciprocal rank, average precision, mean average precision, DCG, and nDCG from ordered judgments with correct denominators.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Relevance Judgment Policy
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 5172 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate precision, recall, reciprocal rank, graded gain, cutoff, and judgment assumptions; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-retrieval-comparison-uncertainty-1at540
# Competency: rag-retrieval-comparison-uncertainty.
# Case 4712: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def evidence_rag_retrieval_comparison_uncertainty_1at540(relevant_retrieved: int = 3, retrieved: int = 5, relevant_total: int = 4, reciprocal_rank: float = 0.5, graded_gain: float = 0.8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1at540 = "1at540-045ta1-4712"
    assert evidence_variant_1at540
    evidence_axes_1at540 = (
        "corpus1at540",
        "query045ta1",
        "tenant4712",
        "index1at5404712",
        "prompt045ta14712",
        "evaluation47121at540",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1at540)) == 6
    if min(relevant_retrieved, retrieved, relevant_total) < 0:
        return False, 0.0, "negative-count"
    if relevant_retrieved > retrieved or relevant_retrieved > relevant_total:
        return False, 0.0, "judgment-count"
    if not 0.0 <= reciprocal_rank <= 1.0 or not 0.0 <= graded_gain <= 1.0:
        return False, 0.0, "metric-range"
    precision = relevant_retrieved / retrieved if retrieved else 0.0
    recall = relevant_retrieved / relevant_total if relevant_total else 0.0
    balanced = 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall) if precision + recall else 0.0
    assert 0.0 <= balanced <= 1.0
    return True, round((balanced + reciprocal_rank + graded_gain) / 3, 4), "metric-vector"
